title: Automated DNA Extraction from Sediment Samples and Filter Membranes Using Magnetic Beads
author: Qianyue Ji

steps_raw.instruction: Use 1 g of each sediment sample or one half of each filter membrane.
Sediment samples were centrifuged in 96-well deep-well plates (3960, Corning) at 12.000 rpm, °C, for 00:05:00 before the extraction, and the supernatant was discarded before extraction.
Filter membranes were cut to small pieces and subjected to extraction directly.
For one reaction, every 20 µL Magnetic Beads-T are mixed with 300 µL PFB-M, and mix them thoroughly.

Take out 5 U-bottom deep-well plates (MGI, 1000004644), and mark them as 【PFBM + Magnetic beads-T】, 【PW1-M】, 【PW2】, and 【PB】respectively. Add reagents according to Table. Item Volume/Well 1 PFBM + Magnetic beads-T 320 μL (PFB-M + Magnetic beads-T mixed liquor) + 240 μL sample 2 PW1-M 400 μL 3 PW2 800 μL 4 PB 120 μL Input volume of PFB-M, PW1-M, PW2, Magnetic Beads-T, and PB
Select script provided by the supplier for DNA extraction.
The extracted DNA can be stored at -20 °C .
Follow the on-screen instructions to place the consumables, samples, and reagents. Confirm the placement and close the door.
